title: paeonia officinalis subsp. corallina
intro: Paeonia officinalis subsp. corallina, also known as the pink peony or coral peony, is a subspecies of the perennial peony plant, belonging to the Paeoniaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as paeonol and paeoniflorin. The medicinal properties of Paeonia officinalis subsp. corallina have been found to provide various health benefits, including relief from menstrual cramps, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
